title: Parent reference, child object — whose method runs?

options: It won't compile — the types don't match
mcq.explanation_md: For overridden methods, Java looks at the **object's actual type at runtime** — here a `Student` — so the Student's version runs. This is runtime polymorphism (dynamic dispatch). The tempting answer is "Member ID", reasoning from the reference type `CollegeMember` — but the reference type only decides which methods you're *allowed to call*, not which version executes. And the assignment compiles fine: a Student *is a* CollegeMember, so a parent reference can always hold a child object. If you got this right without running it, you already understand the heart of OOP in Java.
